name: razor-a1-tagged-int-5
note: the tagged $int (single-key, string payload) decodes to the INTEGER 5 -> canonical `5`.

name: razor-a2-literal-dollar-int-key-object
note: an OBJECT whose sole literal key is `$int` (via $obj) with the SAME payload "5" -> canonical object `{"$int":"5"}`. Same input text-payload, divergent bytes: the engine must NOT confuse a literal `$int` key with the integer tag.

name: razor-a3-dollar-int-numeric-payload-rejects
note: the $int tag payload MUST be a decimal STRING; a bare JSON number payload is rejected (not silently coerced).

name: bigint-b3-2pow53-plus-1
note: 2^53+1 is unrepresentable as an IEEE-754 double (would collide with 2^53); its distinct canonical bytes from b2 prove the engine keeps $int exact, not float.

name: deep-nesting-c1-exceeds-serde-recursion-rejects
note: 300-deep JSON array; serde_json's parser recursion ceiling is 128, so the ENGINE rejects at parse. Must be a clean rejection, never a partial/mis-digested accept.
